There is no information available on the Diemisa capitata plant. However, I'll assume you are referring to the plant Dimorphandra capitata. 

Dimorphandra capitata, also known as Diemisa capitata or Dimorphandra, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family, commonly known as the pea or legume family. The Dimorphandra plant is rich in health benefits, including its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dimorphandra include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Dimorphandra is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its most common preparations include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including high blood pressure and fever.
